mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing starts above 1400 units signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in the unemployment rate below 6.5% signals a tightening labor market that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in the federal funds rate and decline in the consumer price index signals a monetary tightening cycle that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in > 40% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in industrial production above 100 signals a strengthening supply environment that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A joint increase in housing starts and decline in the unemployment rate signals a strengthening demand environment that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 1400 units and UNRATE falls < 6.5%
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in > 50% of triggered instances despite positive signal.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in payroll employment above 140000 signals a strengthening labor market that boosts S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in strengthening periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
